text: The laparoscopic view shows an inflammatory region surrounding the gastro-jugular anastomosis. The surgeon evacuates purulent fluid from the abdomen and pelvis as the first step in managing the perforated marginal ulcer.

The surgeon carefully dissects inflammatory adhesions from the liver to expose the perforation site, which is less than one centimeter in diameter. An omental flap is mobilized and secured over the perforation using two O-vicryl sutures.
The surgeon secures the omental patch over the perforation site without closing the hole first, to avoid enlarging the perforation.
The surgeon places a JP drain in the region after inspecting the omental patch, completing the laparoscopic repair of the perforated marginal ulcer.
